---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification

| Route Path                     | Function Name            | HTTP Method(s) | Template File        | Context Variables Passed (Name : Type)                                  | POST Input Expectations                                     |
|--------------------------------|--------------------------|----------------|----------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| /                              | root_redirect             | GET            | Redirects to /dashboard | None                                                                    | None                                                       |
| /dashboard                    | dashboard                | GET            | dashboard.html        | featured_jobs : list[dict] (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, location:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int), latest_jobs : list[dict] (similar structure) | None                                                       |
| /jobs                        | job_listings             | GET            | job_listings.html     | jobs : list[dict] (job_id:int, title:str, company_id:int, company_name:str, location:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int, category:str), categories : list[str], locations : list[str] | None                                                       |
| /job/<int:job_id>          | job_details              | GET            | job_details.html      | job : dict (job_id:int, title:str, company_id:int, company_name:str, location:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int, description:str), company : dict(company_id:int, company_name:str) | None                                                       |
| /apply/<int:job_id>       | application_form         | GET, POST      | application_form.html | job : dict (job_id:int, title:str), error_message : str (only on GET if any error), success_message : str (only on POST success)              | applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), resume (file), cover_letter (str) |
| /applications                | application_tracking     | GET            | application_tracking.html | applications : list[dict] (application_id:int, job_title:str, company_name:str, status:str, applied_date:str), statuses : list[str] | None                                                       |
| /application/<int:application_id> | application_details      | GET            | application_details.html | application : dict (application_id:int, job_title:str, company_name:str, applicant_name:str, applicant_email:str, status:str, applied_date:str) | None                                                       |
| /companies                  | companies_directory      | GET            | companies.html        | companies : list[dict] (company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str, employee_count:int)                                       | None                                                       |
| /company/<int:company_id>    | company_profile          | GET            | company_profile.html  | company : dict (company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str, location:str, description:str), company_jobs : list[dict] (job_id:int, title:str, status:str) | None                                                       |
| /resumes                    | resume_management        | GET            | resumes.html          | resumes : list[dict] (resume_id:int, applicant_name:str, applicant_email:str, filename:str, upload_date:str, summary:str)            | None                                                       |
| /upload_resume              | upload_resume            | POST           | Redirects to /resumes  | None                                                                     | resume_file (file), applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), summary (str) |
| /delete_resume/<int:resume_id> | delete_resume            | POST           | Redirects to /resumes  | None                                                                     | No form fields, resume_id from URL                         |
| /search                    | search_results           | GET            | search_results.html   | query : str, job_results : list[dict] (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, location:str), company_results : list[dict] (company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str) | query (str) from query parameter                          |

## Section 2: HTML Template Specifications

### 1. templates/dashboard.html
- Page Title: Job Board Dashboard
- Element IDs:
  - dashboard-page (Div): Container for the dashboard page
  - featured-jobs (Div): Display featured job recommendations
  - browse-jobs-button (Button): Navigate to job listings page
  - my-applications-button (Button): Navigate to applications tracking page
  - companies-button (Button): Navigate to companies directory page
- Context Variables:
  - featured_jobs: list of dicts with keys: job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int)
  - latest_jobs: list of dicts, similar keys as featured_jobs
- Navigation Mappings:
  - browse-jobs-button: url_for('job_listings')
  - my-applications-button: url_for('application_tracking')
  - companies-button: url_for('companies_directory')

### 2. templates/job_listings.html
- Page Title: Job Listings
- Element IDs:
  - listings-page (Div): Container for the listings page
  - search-input (Input): Search jobs by title, company, or location
  - category-filter (Dropdown): Filter by job category
  - location-filter (Dropdown): Filter by location
  - jobs-grid (Div): Grid displaying job cards
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }} (Button): View job details button for each job
- Context Variables:
  - jobs: list of dicts with keys: job_id (int), title (str), company_id (int), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int), category (str)
  - categories: list of category names (str)
  - locations: list of locations (str) (e.g., Remote, On-site, Hybrid)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_details', job_id=job.job_id)

### 3. templates/job_details.html
- Page Title: Job Details
- Element IDs:
  - job-details-page (Div): Container for job details page
  - job-title (H1): Display job title
  - company-name (Div): Display company name
  - job-description (Div): Display full job description and requirements
  - salary-range (Div): Display salary range
  - apply-now-button (Button): Button to apply for the job
- Context Variables:
  - job: dict with keys: job_id (int), title (str), company_id (int), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int), description (str)
  - company: dict with keys: company_id (int), company_name (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - apply-now-button: url_for('application_form', job_id=job.job_id)

### 4. templates/application_form.html
- Page Title: Submit Application
- Element IDs:
  - application-form-page (Div): Container for the application form
  - applicant-name (Input): Field for applicant name
  - applicant-email (Input): Field for applicant email
  - resume-upload (File Input): Field to upload resume file
  - cover-letter (Textarea): Field for cover letter text
  - submit-application-button (Button): Submit application button
- Context Variables:
  - job: dict with keys: job_id (int), title (str)
  - error_message: str (optional, only present if errors)
  - success_message: str (optional, only present on success)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - No specific navigation buttons defined

### 5. templates/application_tracking.html
- Page Title: My Applications
- Element IDs:
  - tracking-page (Div): Container for tracking page
  - applications-table (Table): Table showing applications
  - status-filter (Dropdown): Filter applications by status
  - view-application-button-{{ application.application_id }} (Button): View application details button
  - back-to-dashboard (Button): Navigate back to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - applications: list of dicts: application_id (int), job_title (str), company_name (str), status (str), applied_date (str)
  - statuses: list of str - ['All', 'Applied', 'Under Review', 'Interview', 'Rejected']
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-application-button-{{ application.application_id }}: url_for('application_details', application_id=application.application_id)
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard')

### 6. templates/companies.html
- Page Title: Company Directory
- Element IDs:
  - companies-page (Div): Container for companies directory
  - companies-list (Div): List of company cards
  - search-company-input (Input): Search companies by name or industry
  - view-company-button-{{ company.company_id }} (Button): View company profile
  - back-to-dashboard (Button): Navigate back to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - companies: list of dicts: company_id (int), company_name (str), industry (str), employee_count (int)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-company-button-{{ company.company_id }}: url_for('company_profile', company_id=company.company_id)
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard')

### 7. templates/company_profile.html
- Page Title: Company Profile
- Element IDs:
  - company-profile-page (Div): Container for company profile page
  - company-info (Div): Display company name, industry, location, description
  - company-jobs (Div): Container for company's open jobs
  - jobs-list (Div): List of jobs with titles and status indicators
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }} (Button): View job details button
  - back-to-companies (Button): Navigate back to companies directory
- Context Variables:
  - company: dict with keys: company_id (int), company_name (str), industry (str), location (str), description (str)
  - company_jobs: list of dicts: job_id (int), title (str), status (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_details', job_id=job.job_id)
  - back-to-companies: url_for('companies_directory')

### 8. templates/resumes.html
- Page Title: My Resumes
- Element IDs:
  - resume-page (Div): Container for resume page
  - resumes-list (Div): List of uploaded resumes
  - upload-resume-button (Button): Button to upload new resume
  - resume-file-input (File Input, hidden): Hidden file input for upload
  - delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }} (Button): Delete resume button for each resume
  - back-to-dashboard (Button): Navigate back to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - resumes: list of dicts: resume_id (int), applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), filename (str), upload_date (str), summary (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - upload-resume-button: triggers hidden resume-file-input's file selection
  - delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }}: posts to /delete_resume/{{ resume.resume_id }}
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard')

### 9. templates/search_results.html
- Page Title: Search Results
- Element IDs:
  - search-results-page (Div): Container for search results page
  - search-query-display (Div): Display the entered search query
  - results-tabs (Div): Tabs to switch between job and company results
  - job-results (Div): Displays job search results
  - company-results (Div): Displays company search results
  - no-results-message (Div): Shows message if no results
- Context Variables:
  - query: str - The search query text
  - job_results: list of dicts: job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str)
  - company_results: list of dicts: company_id (int), company_name (str), industry (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - Buttons or links to job details: url_for('job_details', job_id=job.job_id)
  - Buttons or links to company profiles: url_for('company_profile', company_id=company.company_id)

---

## Section 3: Data File Schemas

### 1. data/jobs.txt
- Pipe-Delimited Fields:
  1. job_id (int) - Unique identifier for the job
  2. title (str) - Job title
  3. company_id (int) - ID of the company offering the job
  4. location (str) - Job location
  5. salary_min (int) - Minimum salary offered
  6. salary_max (int) - Maximum salary offered
  7. category (str) - Job category
  8. description (str) - Full job description and requirements
  9. posted_date (str, YYYY-MM-DD) - Date the job was posted
- Description: Stores all job postings with details
- Example Rows:
```
1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|80000|120000|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15
2|Data Analyst|2|New York, NY|60000|85000|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16
3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles, CA|50000|70000|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14
```

### 2. data/companies.txt
- Pipe-Delimited Fields:
  1. company_id (int) - Unique identifier for the company
  2. company_name (str) - Name of the company
  3. industry (str) - Industry sector
  4. location (str) - Company location
  5. employee_count (int) - Number of employees
  6. description (str) - Company description
- Description: Stores all registered companies and their profiles
- Example Rows:
```
1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco, CA|500|Leading software solutions provider
2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York, NY|300|Innovative financial services company
3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles, CA|200|Premier healthcare management organization
```

### 3. data/categories.txt
- Pipe-Delimited Fields:
  1. category_id (int) - Unique identifier for the category
  2. category_name (str) - Name of the category
  3. description (str) - Description of the category
- Description: Stores job categories definitions
- Example Rows:
```
1|Technology|Software, IT, and tech-related positions
2|Finance|Banking, accounting, and finance positions
3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry positions
```

### 4. data/applications.txt
- Pipe-Delimited Fields:
  1. application_id (int) - Unique identifier for the application
  2. job_id (int) - ID of the job applied for
  3. applicant_name (str) - Name of the applicant
  4. applicant_email (str) - Email of the applicant
  5. status (str) - Current application status
  6. applied_date (str, YYYY-MM-DD) - Date applied
  7. resume_id (int) - ID for the uploaded resume used
- Description: Stores all job applications submitted
- Example Rows:
```
1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Review|2025-01-17|1
2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2
3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3
```

### 5. data/resumes.txt
- Pipe-Delimited Fields:
  1. resume_id (int) - Unique identifier for the resume
  2. applicant_name (str) - Name of the applicant
  3. applicant_email (str) - Email of the applicant
  4. filename (str) - Filename of the uploaded resume
  5. upload_date (str, YYYY-MM-DD) - Date uploaded
  6. summary (str) - Short professional summary
- Description: Stores resumes uploaded by users
- Example Rows:
```
1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experience
2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial background
3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience
```

### 6. data/job_categories.txt
- Pipe-Delimited Fields:
  1. mapping_id (int) - Unique identifier for mapping
  2. job_id (int) - Job ID
  3. category_id (int) - Category ID
- Description: Maps jobs to categories
- Example Rows:
```
1|1|1
2|2|2
3|3|3
```

---

This completes the comprehensive design specification document for the JobBoard web application, enabling Backend and Frontend developers to implement the project independently and in coordination.

### 1. data/jobs.txt

  6. salary_max (int): Maximum salary

  8. description (str): Full job description

```
1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|80000|120000|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15

  2. company_name (str): Company name
  3. industry (str): Industry sector
  4. location (str): Company location
  5. employee_count (int): Number of employees
  6. description (str): Company description
- Description: Stores all registered companies.
- Example Rows:
```
1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco, CA|500|Leading software solutions provider

  1. category_id (int): Unique category identifier

- Example Rows:
```
1|Technology|Software, IT, and tech-related positions
2|Finance|Banking, accounting, and finance positions
3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry positions
```

### 4. data/applications.txt
- Pipe-Delimited Fields:
  1. application_id (int): Unique application identifier
  2. job_id (int): Job applied for
  3. applicant_name (str): Applicant name
  4. applicant_email (str): Applicant email
  5. status (str): Application status
  6. applied_date (str, format YYYY-MM-DD): Date applied

- Description: Stores job applications.
- Example Rows:
```
1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Review|2025-01-17|1
2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2
3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3
```

### 5. data/resumes.txt

  3. applicant_email (str): Email of applicant
  4. filename (str): Uploaded resume filename
  5. upload_date (str, format YYYY-MM-DD): Date of upload

- Description: Stores uploaded resumes.

1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experience
2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial background
3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience

```
1|1|1
